mission.title: podman-container-tools: [Bug]: podman build --secrets with environment variables does not work on MacOS
mission.description: [Bug]: podman build --secrets with environment variables does not work on MacOS. This issue affects 16+ users.

steps.title: Identify podman-container-tools troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your podman-container-tools installation:
```bash
podman --version
podman info --format json | jq .version
```
Look for errors or warnings that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review podman-container-tools configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant podman-container-tools configuration:
### Issue Description

Trying to pass secrets into podman build on MacOS does not function as expected
```
podman build --secret id=my_secret,env=MY_SECRET ls -l /run/secrets/my_secret
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Bug]: podman build --secrets with environment variables…
steps.description:
## 

## Testing using a shell

Create a `Dockerfile` with the following content
```
FROM python:3.9-slim-bullseye
RUN --mount=type=secret,id=mysecret echo "Secret is" && cat /run/secrets/mysecret
```
Create a `secrets.txt` file with the following content
```
filebasedsecret
```
Add an environment
```yaml
podman build --secret id=my_secret,env=MY_SECRET ls -l /run/secrets/my_secret
```

steps.title: Confirm [Bug]: podman build --secrets with environment… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
Test podman-container-tools to confirm the issue is resolved.
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
## 

## Testing using a shell

Create a `Dockerfile` with the following content
```
FROM python:3.9-slim-bullseye
RUN --mount=type=secret,id=mysecret echo "Secret is" && cat /run/secrets/mysecret
```
Create a `secrets.txt` file with the following content
```
filebasedsecret
```
Add an environment variable
`TEST_VAR=envvarsecret`

Once you have these files, run the following in your
codeSnippets:
$ podman info
host:
  arch: arm64
  buildahVersion: 1.28.0
  cgroupControllers:
  - cpu
  - io
  - memory
  - pids
  cgroupManager: systemd
  cgroupVersion: v2
  conmon:
    package: conmon-2.1.5-1.fc37.aarch64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.1.5, commit: '
  cpuUtilization:
    idlePercent: 98.84
    systemPercent: 0.66
    userPercent: 0.5
  cpus: 2
  distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    variant: coreos
    version: "37"
  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: localhost.localdomain
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 1000000
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 503
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 1000000
  kerne

prerequisites.description: A working podman-container-tools installation or development environment.
